Zewail City Revives the Cultural Salon
Zewail City of Science, Technology and Innovation has announced the relaunch of its Cultural Salon after several years of pause.
Dr Wael Akl, the Executive President of Zewail City, explained that the goal is to highlight inspiring Egyptian figures and role models of soft power in culture, science, politics, society, economy, and sports.
The first season of the salon featured leading scientists and thinkers in discussions with students and researchers, including Dr Magdi Yacoub, Dr Mohamed Ghoneim, Dr Ahmed Okasha (all founding board members alongside Dr Ahmed Zewail), as well as Dr Mostafa El-Sayed and Dr Hassan Abou El-Einen, former director of the Mansoura University Kidney Center.
Dr Wael Akl stressed that the return of the salon comes at a time when Egypt is taking steps toward building an integrated society, underlining the importance of passing on the experiences of such figures to younger generations as role models. He also noted that Zewail City, with its advanced infrastructure, has achieved significant progress in education and scientific research despite the passing of its founder, Nobel Laureate Ahmed Zewail.
The first guest of the new season will be announced soon, followed by a series of Egypt’s most prominent figures across various fields. The City will also open its doors to the public and visitors to take part in this cultural event.
30, Nov 2025
We are pleased to share that Aquila ZC, Zewail City's robotics team, has won the VEX U World Championship Judges Award 2025 in Dallas, Texas!
Zewail City of Science, Technology and Innovation proudly announces that Aquila ZC, our official robotics team, has won the prestigious VEX U ROBOTICS WORLD CHAMPIONSHIP JUDGES AWARD at the 2025 VEX U World Championship held in Dallas, Texas, USA held from May 6-14, 2025.
This remarkable achievement marks the first time an Egyptian university team has not only qualified but also won an award at this global competition, placing Egypt prominently on the world robotics map!
The Judges Award is a distinct honor that recognizes exceptional attributes including outstanding communication skills, exemplary teamwork, consistent professionalism, and a robust student-centered approach throughout this demanding competition. Competing against over 100 elite university teams from more than 50 countries at the world's largest robotics competition (recognized by Guinness World Records), our team demonstrated that Zewail City students can excel among the brightest engineering minds from prestigious universities worldwide.
Our victorious team includes talented students from diverse engineering programs:
Communications and Information Engineering:
Ziad Behairy
Nourhan Mohamed
Mohamed Morshedy
Amr Ashraf
Renewable Energy Engineering:
Mohamed Ahmed Ibrahim
Ziad Reda
Mada Zohdy
Aya Makram
Aerospace Engineering:
Mariam Badawy
Yahya Hazem
We extend our sincere appreciation to Dr. Ahmed Sayed Abd-Rabou, Visiting Assistant Professor in the Nanotechnology and Nanoelectronics Engineering Program, for his distinguished academic supervision.
This victory highlights the exceptional quality of education at Zewail City and demonstrates how collaboration across different programs can achieve international excellence. It reflects our institution's commitment to fostering innovation and preparing students to compete at the highest global levels.
18, May 2025
Egyptian Participation in CMS Experiment Wins 2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics
Egyptian Participation in CMS Experiment Wins 2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics
The CMS experiment at CERN’s Large Hadron Collider (LHC), in which Egypt has been actively involved since 2010, has been awarded the prestigious 2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics. This prize recognizes the experiment’s groundbreaking contributions, including the discovery of the Higgs boson in 2012 and ongoing research beyond the Standard Model.
Egypt’s participation is coordinated through the Egyptian Network for High Energy Physics (ENHEP), led by Dr. Shabaan Khalil from Zewail City for Science, Technology, and Innovation. Several Egyptian researchers, including Dr. Ahmed Ali Abdel-Aleem and Dr. Amr Mohamed, have played key roles in data analysis, detector development, and scientific leadership within CMS.
The prize celebrates the collective efforts of over 5,700 scientists worldwide and underscores Egypt’s growing impact on global physics research.
For more details, visit:
20, May 2025
Zewail City of Science and Technology is proud to announce that Professor Mohamed Farhat Othman
Zewail City of Science and Technology is proud to announce that Professor Mohamed Farhat Othman, Professor and Director of the Nanotechnology and Nanoelectronics Engineering Program and Director of the Center for Nanotechnology, has been elected as a Fellow of the African Academy of Sciences (FAAS).
Founded in 1985, the AAS promotes excellence in science and technology across Africa.
Prof. Farhat’s membership in the African Academy of Sciences (AAS) recognizes his outstanding research achievements in the field of nanotechnology and nanoelectronics.
We warmly congratulate him on this remarkable achievement.

Zewail City’s Centre for Gen ...
Zewail City’s Centre for Genomics was honored to host Dr. Lucy Weinert from the University of Cambridge for an inspiring session on evolutionary biology and comparative genomics. Her insights into One Health genomics, infectious diseases, and host-jumping dynamics sparked meaningful discussions and opened doors for future research collaborations.
Prof. Ibrahim El-Sherbiny Aw ...
"Zewail City of Science, Technology and Innovation is pleased to announce that Professor Dr. Ibrahim El-Sherbiny, the Founding Director of the Nanoscience Program and Director of the Center for Materials Science (CMS) at Zewail City, has been awarded the Doctor of Science (D.Sc) degree from Mansoura University. The Doctor of Science (DSC) is an advanced academic degree awarded in recognition of substantial and sustained contributions to scientific knowledge and research. It is often considered a higher doctorate, highly above the level of conventional PhD, and is typically awarded to individuals who have demonstrated a significant international impact on their field.
The research and the overall scientific activity of Professor El-Sherbiny were evaluated by an elite group of British scientists, from prestigious English universities such as University of Cambridge, University of Birmingham, etc, who are experts in nanotechnology and nanopolymer fields, and all of them hold the same esteemed DSc degree:
1- Sir Prof. Mark Welland (FRS, FREng, FIET, FInstP), Head of Nanoscience Centre, University of Cambridge, UK
2- Prof. James Hay (DPhil, DSc, FRSC), University of Birmingham, UK
3- Prof. Andrew Lowe (DPhil, DSc, FRSC), (Currently in a sabbatical at KU, UAE)"
The First Egyptian Researche ...
"Kuwait Science Club announced the winners of the 14th International Invention Fair in the Middle East (IIFME), held in Kuwait under His Highness Amir Sheikh Mishal Al-Ahmad's patronage, drawing 200 participants from 40 Arab and Foreign countries.
The IIFME's grand prize was given to the Egyptian inventor Dr. Ibrahim El-Sherbiny, the Founding Director of the Nanoscience Program and the Director of the Material Science Research Center in Zewail City, for his first invention in nanotechnology for continuous vasolidation, registered in his name and the world-famous Egyptian Dr. Magdi Yacoub, and for his second invention for environment-friendly, long-lasting, biodegradable urea fertilizer."
Zewail City Luminary Dr. Sal ...
In a significant stride towards academic excellence, Zewail City proudly announces the distinguished achievement of Dr. Salah Obayya, a visionary force in the realm of photonics research and development. His exceptional contributions to the field have not only been recognized but also celebrated by the renowned Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE).
At the forefront of a recent IEEE feature article titled "Globalization in Photonics Research and Development," Prof. Obayya emerges as a key figure, recognized for his groundbreaking work in computational photonics. This esteemed profile showcases his 30 years of pioneering research, blending modern computational modeling and artificial intelligence techniques to comprehend and address intricate nanophotonic structures and devices.
The article highlights his profound impact across various domains, including optical communications, plasmonics, and metamaterials. The culmination of his efforts has rightfully earned him an award from the esteemed IEEE Society, a testament to his brilliance and dedication.
Professor Obayya, the Director-General of Research at Zewail City, the Founding Director of the Center for Photonics and Smart Materials (CPSM), and an Honorary Professor at the University of Nottingham stands as an esteemed member of the Zewail City community. His journey reflects the vision and values instilled by the late Dr. Ahmed Zewail, whom he reveres as a role model.
This achievement stands as a beacon of success, symbolizing the continuous dedication of Zewail City's faculty to pushing the boundaries of knowledge and making impactful contributions to their respective fields. The entire Zewail City community congratulates Prof. Salah Obayya for this well-deserved recognition, further solidifying the institution's commitment to excellence and innovation.
The International Artificial ...
The International Cooperation Center at the International Artificial Intelligence Industry Alliance (AIIA) in Hong Kong, extended a formal invitation to Professor Salah Obayya, Zewail City’ Director General of Research, Founder and Director of Center for Photonics and Smart Materials (CPSM), and the former Vice-Chair for Academic Affairs to join their esteemed alliance, as one of the world's foremost industry scientists. This invitation comes in recognition of his pioneering research contributions to AI photonics and its impact on both the international scientific and industrial communities.
Following a thorough screening process by the Alliance, Prof. Obayya's selection comes as an international recognition of his exceptional contributions to the field of Artificial Intelligence in Photonics, positioning him as a distinguished addition to the AIIA. The AIIA Board Members welcomed Prof. Obayya, anticipating his invaluable contributions to the industrialization of AI.
The International Artificial Intelligence Industry Alliance (AIIA) is a leading global consortium of eminent scientists and researchers dedicated to advancing the field of artificial intelligence. Headquartered in Hong Kong, the alliance focuses on collaborative efforts, knowledge exchange, and research initiatives that shape the future of AI.
Professor Obayya appointed e ...
"Springer Nature; the global publishing house, announced the appointment of Professor Salah
Obayya; Acting Director General for Research, Director of Zewail City’s Center for Photonics and Smart Materials and former Vice-
Chair for Academic Affairs as the incoming editor-in-chief of Optical and Quantum Electronics
(OQEL).
OQEL is considered one of Springer Nature’s top achieving journals, and is internationally
recognized as one of the leading journals in the fields of optics, photonics and quantum
electronics.
Based on Springer Nature’s standards, the Editor-in-Chief is expected to be a world-leading
researcher with a wide scope and an excellent publication record in the fields of optical and
quantum electronics. The Editor-in-Chief will continue to pro-actively develop the journal both
in scope and impact."
Professor Salah Obayya elect ...
Professor Salah Obayya, Director of Zewail City’s Center for Photonics and Smart Materials and former Vice-Chair for Academic Affairs, has been elected a Fellow of the Asia Pacific Artificial Intelligence Association (AAIA), the highest level of membership in the association.
According to the association, Obayya was selected for his “outstanding achievements in the
area of intelligent computational modeling of modern nanophotonic devices.”
The AAIA fellows come from the world’s top universities, including Stanford University,
Princeton University, MIT, California Institute of Technology, Cornell University, as well as the
world’s top scientists in AI related fields.
Zewail City research team ar ...
"A research article entitled ""SARS-CoV-2 potential drugs, drug targets, and biomarkers: a viral-host interaction network-based analysis"" published by Dr. Eman Badr, Assistant Professor of Computational Biology and Genomics in Zewail City's Biomedical Sciences Program was chosen among Scientific Reports’ Top 100 in Chemistry - 2022.
In March 2023, Scientific Reports announced the Top 100 in Chemistry - 2022 collection. This collection highlights the most downloaded chemistry research papers published in 2022. Featuring authors from all over the world, this collection highlights valuable research from an international community.

Academics
About UST
At the heart of Zewail City is the University of Science and Technology (UST). Built around dissolving the boundaries between various disciplines, UST offers interdisciplinary academic programs with a dynamic learning environment. UST academic programs have been designed to cope with the developing needs of the society and to meet the requirements of the fourth industrial revolution. The University offers undergraduate and graduate degrees in emerging areas of engineering, science, computational sciences, artificial intelligence, and business The University accepted the first cohort in Fall 2013, and classes have been graduating since Summer 2017.
Undergraduate Studies
Undergraduate programs offered by the University of Science and Technology (UST) at Zewail City prepares their graduates not only for technical expertise but also for success in today's dynamic world. UST's undergraduate programs cultivate critical thinking and practical skills through a comprehensive curriculum. Graduates develop the ability to identify and solve complex technical and scientific problems. They gain expertise in conducting research, designing experiments, testing hypotheses, and analyzing data. Effective communication is crucial in any field, and UST programs emphasize the ability to clearly share ideas and collaborate effectively with others.
Graduate Studies
Building upon the strong foundation laid by their undergraduate studies, postgraduate programs at the University of Science and Technology (UST) at Zewail City propel graduates to the forefront of their chosen fields. Designed to cultivate advanced research capabilities and professional expertise, these programs empower students to tackle complex scientific and technical challenges. Through rigorous coursework and research opportunities, UST graduate programs equip students with the ability to design and conduct independent research, analyze and interpret complex data, and effectively communicate their findings to both academic and professional audiences.

Undergraduate Studies
Studying at the University of Science and Technology
Undergraduate programs offered by the University of Science and Technology (UST) at Zewail City prepares their graduates not only for technical expertise but also for success in today's dynamic world. UST's undergraduate programs cultivate critical thinking and practical skills through a comprehensive curriculum. Graduates develop the ability to identify and solve complex technical and scientific problems. They gain expertise in conducting research, designing experiments, testing hypotheses, and analyzing data. Effective communication is crucial in any field, and UST programs emphasize the ability to clearly share ideas and collaborate effectively with others. Furthermore, graduates learn to assess the broader impact of their work, considering global, economic, environmental, and societal factors. UST fosters adaptability by incorporating a multidisciplinary approach, allowing graduates to thrive in ever-changing technological landscapes. The culminating capstone project provides a platform for graduates to apply their acquired knowledge by designing and implementing a solution, demonstrating their ability to translate theory into practical applications. In essence, UST equips students to become well-rounded graduates, technically proficient and prepared to lead and innovate in their chosen fields.

UST Minors
A student enrolled in any UST undergraduate program can optionally join any offered Minor by UST, as long as the Minor is not in the same field as his/her Major, even if offered by a different school.
Registering for Minor courses follows UST's regular course registration policies and regulations.

Academics
Graduate Academic & Professional Studies
Building upon the strong foundation laid by their undergraduate studies, postgraduate programs at the University of Science and Technology (UST) at Zewail City propel graduates to the forefront of their chosen fields. Designed to cultivate advanced research capabilities and professional expertise, these programs empower students to tackle complex scientific and technical challenges. Through rigorous coursework and research opportunities, UST graduate programs equip students with the ability to design and conduct independent research, analyze and interpret complex data, and effectively communicate their findings to both academic and professional audiences. The emphasis on critical thinking fosters the ability to solve real-world problems and develop innovative solutions that consider not only technical feasibility but also broader economic, environmental, and societal implications. UST fosters a collaborative learning environment, encouraging students to work effectively within research teams and develop strong leadership skills. By the time of graduation, UST graduate students emerge as highly skilled researchers and professionals, prepared to make significant contributions to their chosen fields and drive advancements in science and technology.

Research
Research at Zewail City
Welcome to the esteemed realm of research at Zewail City of Science and Technology. Delve into the forefront of scientific inquiry, where our distinguished faculty and researchers spearhead transformative endeavors across a diverse array of disciplines.
With a steadfast commitment to excellence and a spirit of collaboration, we undertake pioneering explorations in nanotechnology, biotechnology, and beyond. Our state-of-the-art facilities and unwavering dedication to scholarly pursuit empower us to confront the most pressing challenges of our time. Embark on a journey of intellectual discovery and societal impact by exploring our esteemed research endeavors. Join us as we pave the path toward a future shaped by innovation and progress.
Research Institutes
Helmy Institute for Medical Sciences (HIMS)
Helmy Institute for Medical Sciences (HIMS) is a leading research institution dedicated to advancing health and medical knowledge through a multidisciplinary approach. It encompasses 5 specialized centers, including :
**The Center for Aging and Associated Diseases (CAAD)**, **the Center for Genomics (CG)**, **the Center for Microbiology and Phage Therapy**, **the Center of Excellence for Stem Cells and Regenerative Medicine**, **the Center of Scientific Excellence for Food Research and Analysis (cSEFRA)**
Together, these centers position HIMS at the forefront of medical research and innovation, contributing significantly to societal health and well-being.
NBE Institute for Nanoscience and Informatics (INI)
NBE Institute for Nanoscience and Informatics (INI) is a multidisciplinary research hub dedicated to advancing the fields of nanoscience and nanotechnology. It encompasses several specialized centers, including :
**The Center for Materials Science(CMS), the Center for Nanoelectronics and Devices(CND), the Center for Nanotechnology (CNT), additional Center for Photonics and Smart Materials, Lastly the Certified Reference Materials (CRM) Center.**
Together, these centers position INI as a leader in scientific research and technological advancement, contributing to a sustainable and knowledge-based economy.
Institute for Basic Sciences (IBS)
Institute for Basic Sciences (IBS) is a premier research institution dedicated
to advancing the field of theoretical physics. Through its **Center for**
**Fundamental Physics (CFP) established in 2012.**
Institute for Imaging and Visualization (IIV)
Institute for Imaging and Visualization (IIV) is a cutting-edge research
institution dedicated to advancing the fields of imaging and structural
biotechnology. It encompasses **the Center for Imaging and Microscopy (CIM)Additionally, the Center for X-Ray Determination of the Structure of Matter (CXDS)**
These centers position IIV as a leader in imaging technology and structural research , contributing significantly to scientific advancements in Egypt and the global community.
Institute for Energy, Environment and Space (IEES)
The Institute for Energy, Environment, and Space (IEES) is dedicated to accelerating the transition to a sustainable energy system through innovative research, development, and education.
Its Center for Renewable Energy and Energy Efficiency (REEE) works to provide a stronger economy, healthier environment, and sustainable future, empowering communities with clean energy knowledge and practices.

It is often considered a higher doctorate, highly above the level of conventional PhD, and is typically awarded to individuals who have demonstrated a significant international impact on their field.
The research and the overall scientific activity of Professor El-Sherbiny were evaluated by an elite group of British scientists, from prestigious English universities such as University of Cambridge, University of Birmingham, etc, who are experts in nanotechnology and nanopolymer fields, and all of them hold the same esteemed DSc degree:
1- Sir Prof. Mark Welland (FRS, FREng, FIET, FInstP), Head of Nanoscience Centre, University of Cambridge, UK
2- Prof. James Hay (DPhil, DSc, FRSC), University of Birmingham, UK
3- Prof. Andrew Lowe (DPhil, DSc, FRSC), (Currently in a sabbatical at KU, UAE)"

"Kuwait Science Club announced the winners of the 14th International Invention Fair in the Middle East (IIFME), held in Kuwait under His Highness Amir Sheikh Mishal Al-Ahmad's patronage, drawing 200 participants from 40 Arab and Foreign countries.
The IIFME's grand prize was given to the Egyptian inventor Dr. Ibrahim El-Sherbiny, the Founding Director of the Nanoscience Program and the Director of the Material Science Research Center in Zewail City, for his first invention in nanotechnology for continuous vasolidation, registered in his name and the world-famous Egyptian Dr. Magdi Yacoub, and for his second invention for environment-friendly, long-lasting, biodegradable urea fertilizer."
Zewail City Luminary Dr. Sal ...
In a significant stride towards academic excellence, Zewail City proudly announces the distinguished achievement of Dr. Salah Obayya, a visionary force in the realm of photonics research and development. His exceptional contributions to the field have not only been recognized but also celebrated by the renowned Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE).
At the forefront of a recent IEEE feature article titled "Globalization in Photonics Research and Development," Prof. Obayya emerges as a key figure, recognized for his groundbreaking work in computational photonics. This esteemed profile showcases his 30 years of pioneering research, blending modern computational modeling and artificial intelligence techniques to comprehend and address intricate nanophotonic structures and devices.
The article highlights his profound impact across various domains, including optical communications, plasmonics, and metamaterials. The culmination of his efforts has rightfully earned him an award from the esteemed IEEE Society, a testament to his brilliance and dedication.
Professor Obayya, the Director-General of Research at Zewail City, the Founding Director of the Center for Photonics and Smart Materials (CPSM), and an Honorary Professor at the University of Nottingham stands as an esteemed member of the Zewail City community. His journey reflects the vision and values instilled by the late Dr. Ahmed Zewail, whom he reveres as a role model.
This achievement stands as a beacon of success, symbolizing the continuous dedication of Zewail City's faculty to pushing the boundaries of knowledge and making impactful contributions to their respective fields. The entire Zewail City community congratulates Prof. Salah Obayya for this well-deserved recognition, further solidifying the institution's commitment to excellence and innovation.
The International Artificial ...
The International Cooperation Center at the International Artificial Intelligence Industry Alliance (AIIA) in Hong Kong, extended a formal invitation to Professor Salah Obayya, Zewail City’ Director General of Research, Founder and Director of Center for Photonics and Smart Materials (CPSM), and the former Vice-Chair for Academic Affairs to join their esteemed alliance, as one of the world's foremost industry scientists. This invitation comes in recognition of his pioneering research contributions to AI photonics and its impact on both the international scientific and industrial communities.
Following a thorough screening process by the Alliance, Prof. Obayya's selection comes as an international recognition of his exceptional contributions to the field of Artificial Intelligence in Photonics, positioning him as a distinguished addition to the AIIA. The AIIA Board Members welcomed Prof. Obayya, anticipating his invaluable contributions to the industrialization of AI.
The International Artificial Intelligence Industry Alliance (AIIA) is a leading global consortium of eminent scientists and researchers dedicated to advancing the field of artificial intelligence. Headquartered in Hong Kong, the alliance focuses on collaborative efforts, knowledge exchange, and research initiatives that shape the future of AI.

12, Feb 2024
Ms. Ghada Tawfiq, Governor's Advisor for Corporate Social Responsibility at Central Bank of Egypt, Visit to Zewail City of Science and Technology
In the framework of fruitful cooperation between the Central Bank of Egypt and Zewail City of Science, Technology and Innovation, Professor Dr. Mahmoud Abd Rabbo, Acting Executive President of Zewail City, welcomed Ms. Ghada Tawfiq, Governor's Advisor for Corporate Social Responsibility - Central Bank of Egypt.
The visit included a meeting with the innovative young students from the University of Science and Technology in Zewail City, presenting the projects of entrepreneurs from Zewail City graduates, in addition to discussing aspects of future cooperation.
The visit was concluded with a tour inside Zewail City, the Cultural and Administrative Complex, and the new academic building to see and follow all updated developments of the equipped buildings with the latest scientific and laboratory equipment for an integrated student life, and also to follow up on the progress of study and the educational and research facilities in the city.
27, Dec 2023
The International Artificial Intelligence Industry Alliance Invites Obayya as Esteemed Alliance Member
The International Cooperation Center at the International Artificial Intelligence Industry Alliance (AIIA) in Hong Kong, extended a formal invitation to Professor Salah Obayya, Zewail City’ Director General of Research, Founder and Director of Center for Photonics and Smart Materials (CPSM), and the former Vice-Chair for Academic Affairs to join their esteemed alliance, as one of the world's foremost industry scientists. This invitation comes in recognition of his pioneering research contributions to AI photonics and its impact on both the international scientific and industrial communities.
Following a thorough screening process by the Alliance, Prof. Obayya's selection comes as an international recognition of his exceptional contributions to the field of Artificial Intelligence in Photonics, positioning him as a distinguished addition to the AIIA. The AIIA Board Members welcomed Prof. Obayya, anticipating his invaluable contributions to the industrialization of AI.

Headquartered in Hong Kong, the alliance focuses on collaborative efforts, knowledge exchange, and research initiatives that shape the future of AI.

AI for Health Systems Transformation
We are pleased to invite you to the upcoming
AI for Health Systems Transformation: Policy, Partnerships, and Systems Readiness
workshop, hosted by the
Centre for Genomics at Zewail City of Science and Technology
in collaboration with
The Global Health Network (TGHN)
, the
Global Health Institute at the American University of Beirut
, and
HealthAI
Date:
2 July 2025
Location:
Zewail City of Science and Technology, Cairo, Egypt
Format:
Hybrid (In-person & Online)
This full-day workshop will bring together leading experts, policymakers, and innovators from across the MENA regions to explore how Artificial Intelligence can be responsibly integrated into healthcare systems to improve outcomes and equity. Through presentations, panel discussions, and collaborative sessions, we will explore:
Regional dynamics of AI use in healthcare
Policy and ethical challenges
Strategic pathways to strengthen infrastructure, data governance, and capacity
Practical case studies showcasing AI innovations

Fluorescence Microscope in Bioimaging Applications
Workshop:
Fluorescence Microscope in Bioimaging Applications
Zewail City of Science and Technology is organizing a specialized workshop aimed at students and graduates of scientific and medical faculties to explore the applications of fluorescence microscopy in bioimaging.
Location: Center of Excellence for Stem Cell and Regenerative Medicine Research, Helmy Institute for Biomedical Sciences, Zewail City of Science and Technology
In this workshop, you'll Learn:
-Techniques for using fluorescence dyes to study cellular structures
-Identifying precise protein locations and their interactions within the cellular system
-Dynamic imaging of cell movement and interaction
Using image analysis tools to understand and analyze data
Instructors: A team of researchers from the Center of Excellence for Stem Cell and Regenerative Medicine Research
Certificate:
Participants will receive an accredited and stamped certificate from Zewail City of Science and Technology upon completion of the workshop."
Training Fee: 1500 EGP
Important Notes:
Seats are limited and registration is on a first-come, first-served basis.
Participants will be contacted prior to the workshop for attendance coordination.
Don't miss this opportunity!

Basic Animal Cell Culture Techniques
Workshop: Basic Animal Cell Culture Techniques
Zewail City of Science and Technology is pleased to announce a specialized workshop titled "
Basic Animal Cell Culture Techniques
This workshop is designed for students and graduates of scientific and medical colleges, and anyone related to the field
Location: Stem Cell and Regenerative Medicine Excellence Center, Zewail City of Science and Technology, 6th of October City
In this workshop, you'll:
-Learn the basic principles of animal cell culture techniques.
-Study the isolation of stem cells from various sources.
-Conduct experiments to observe how cells interact with different changing factors.
-Gain both theoretical and hands-on practical knowledge of stem cells and other laboratory cell types.
Certificate: participants will receive an accredited and stamped certificate from Zewail City of Science and Technology upon completion of the workshop."
Training Fee: 1500 EGP (only).
Registration:
To apply and get more details, please fill out the registration form here:
Important Notes:
Seats are limited and registration is on a first-come, first-served basis.
Participants will be contacted prior to the workshop for attendance coordination.
Don’t miss out on this valuable opportunity to enhance your skills in stem cell research and animal cell culture!

Bacteriophage Applications to Improve Food Safety and Human Health
The Center of Scientific Excellence for Food Research and Analysis (CSEFRA), internationally accredited to ISO standards, in collaboration with the Science, Technology, and Innovation Funding Authority (STDF), is pleased to announce the organization of a scientific seminar titled:
Bacteriophage Applications to Improve Food Safety and Human Health
Seminar Overview:
This seminar will explore the groundbreaking potential of bacteriophages in enhancing food safety and human health. Topics include:
The latest research on bacteriophage therapy.
Center tour to recognize the state of the art equipment and see the phage research
Applications in combating antibiotic-resistant pathogens.
Potential solutions to pressing global health challenges.
The seminar program features:
-Presentations by leading experts.
-Panel discussions to foster collaboration and innovation.
-A certified participation certificate from Zewail City for all attendees.

UST awards scholarships based on academic merit.
100% for 50 of the top 500 students in Thanaweya Amma (including the highest 2 applicants in the top 10 students of each governorate)
100% for the one highest applicant in the top 10 of each STEM School.

Students Thanaweya Amma, Alazhar, STEM with scores from 80% to below 90% receive a 25% discount of the tuition fees.
Students (Arab, IGCSE, IB, French Baccalaureate, Canadian Diploma, German Abitur certificates holders) with scores of 93% or above receive a 50% discount of the tuition fees.
Students (Arab, IGCSE, IB, French Baccalaureate, Canadian Diploma, German Abitur certificates holders) with scores from 88% to below 93% receive a 25% discount of the tuition fees.
American Diploma students with scores of 113% or above receive a 50% discount of the tuition fees.
American Diploma students with scores from 108% to below 113% receive a 25% discount of the tuition fees.

25% discount for students applying to the School of Science (First Year).

Library
The Library has been integral to its life and scholarly activity since the city was founded. It enjoys a privileged location within the Academic designed building. It consists of two hall's (two ancillary spaces for staff) and affords a pleasant working environment with its view out over the hall’s fine garden.
The Library Collection serves Zewail City members: Students, Faculty, Staff and Fellows of the Halls and makes a distinctive contribution to the Scientific Research in the University. Its Collection mainly consists of Science, Engineering, Energy & Environmental Engineering, Nano Science, Arabic collocation, Computer Science and Prof. Ahmed Zewail personal library.
At present, its stock consists of some 3,500 items and it is open from 08:30 AM - 03:45 PM for Zewail City members.
Library Services:
Circulation.
Renewing borrowing period via email.
Reference Services.
On-line Search using OPAC: Search within the Library Catalogue and locate the books required on the shelves. (On Campus).
User Orientation (how to use the library).
Query Answering.
Holdings: If a book you need is currently checked out you can ask the Librarian to place a hold and to be notified when the book is returned, so you can visit the library to check it out during 2 days or your hold will be cancelled.
Text Matching Software:
The University community has free access to two different web-based originality-checking tools geared to helping ZC members avoid plagiarism. Below is a brief overview of each and how best to use them.
Turnitin and iThenticate require submissions to be made by the sole or first-author of the work. They are not intended for use by second-party individuals to check originality.
Turnitin:
made specifically for classroom use and is intended for reviewing student work. Similar to Blackboard’s Safe Assign service, and can be integrated into Canvas courses and allows instructors to review originality reports of student work. It compares student work against 60 billion web pages; 600 million student papers that have been submitted to the Turnitin database; over 100 million articles from professional journals, periodicals, and publications. Submitted documents are added to a database of material from colleges and universities around the country.
Note:
An instructor must first have the line manager's approval before creating an account.
iThenticate
Note:

Zewail City Revives the Cultur ...
Zewail City of Science, Technology and Innovation has announced the relaunch of its Cultural Salon after several years of pause.
Dr Wael Akl, the Executive President of Zewail City, explained that the goal is to highlight inspiring Egyptian figures and role models of soft power in culture, science, politics, society, economy, and sports.
The first season of the salon featured leading scientists and thinkers in discussions with students and researchers, including Dr Magdi Yacoub, Dr Mohamed Ghoneim, Dr Ahmed Okasha (all founding board members alongside Dr Ahmed Zewail), as well as Dr Mostafa El-Sayed and Dr Hassan Abou El-Einen, former director of the Mansoura University Kidney Center.
Dr Wael Akl stressed that the return of the salon comes at a time when Egypt is taking steps toward building an integrated society, underlining the importance of passing on the experiences of such figures to younger generations as role models. He also noted that Zewail City, with its advanced infrastructure, has achieved significant progress in education and scientific research despite the passing of its founder, Nobel Laureate Ahmed Zewail.
The first guest of the new season will be announced soon, followed by a series of Egypt’s most prominent figures across various fields. The City will also open its doors to the public and visitors to take part in this cultural event.

Zewail City is honored to hos ...
On Thursday, the Zewail City of Science and Technology welcomed an Emirati delegation consisting of a group of astronauts from the United Arab Emirates. The delegation was led by His Excellency Dr. Sultan Al Neyadi, UAE Minister of State for Youth Affairs and the astronaut with the longest stay at the International Space Station, which lasted six months, in addition to the first Emirati astronaut, Hazzaa Al Mansoori, along with astronauts Mohammad Al Mulla and Nora Al Matrooshi.
Dr. Mahmoud Abdrabou, Executive President of Zewail City, made sure to welcome the Emirati delegation, highlighting the strong and distinguished relationship between Egypt and the UAE at all levels. He presented an overview of the components of the city and the university, showcasing the significant scientific achievements made so far.
The Emirati astronauts shared the experience of the Mohammed bin Rashid Space Centre and the country's support for this ambitious scientific project, which led to the participation of the first Arab astronaut from the UAE in an eight-day space mission, followed by Dr. Sultan Al Neyadi’s six-month mission.
The UAE Minister of Youth Affairs praised the great scientific renaissance in Egypt, mentioning the distinguished infrastructure he witnessed during his visit to the New Administrative Capital and several Egyptian institutions. He pointed out that the late Nobel laureate Ahmed Zewail was a unique example of the ability of Egyptians and Arabs to achieve scientific dreams and ambitions. He also acknowledged the contributions of Egyptian scientist Dr. Farouk El-Baz to their space endeavors, expressing his hopes for starting scientific cooperation in the field of space between Egypt and the UAE.
Dr. Al Neyadi added that the educational philosophy at Zewail University and its renowned scientific reputation motivated him and his colleagues to visit and meet the university students. He also appreciated the special relationship between Egypt and the UAE on both official and popular levels.
At the end of the visit, : Dr. Mahmoud Abdrabou، on behalf of Dr. Ayman Ashour, Minister of Higher Education and Chairman of the Zewail City Board of Trustees, presented a commemorative shield to Dr. Al Neyadi in recognition of his scientific contributions.

Egyptian Participation in CMS Experiment Wins 2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics
May 20, 2025
Egyptian Participation in CMS Experiment Wins 2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics
The CMS experiment at CERN’s Large Hadron Collider (LHC), in which Egypt has been actively involved since 2010, has been awarded the prestigious 2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics. This prize recognizes the experiment’s groundbreaking contributions, including the discovery of the Higgs boson in 2012 and ongoing research beyond the Standard Model.
Egypt’s participation is coordinated through the Egyptian Network for High Energy Physics (ENHEP), led by Dr. Shabaan Khalil from Zewail City for Science, Technology, and Innovation. Several Egyptian researchers, including Dr. Ahmed Ali Abdel-Aleem and Dr. Amr Mohamed, have played key roles in data analysis, detector development, and scientific leadership within CMS.
The prize celebrates the collective efforts of over 5,700 scientists worldwide and underscores Egypt’s growing impact on global physics research.

Prof. Ibrahim El-Sherbiny Awar ...
"Zewail City of Science, Technology and Innovation is pleased to announce that Professor Dr. Ibrahim El-Sherbiny, the Founding Director of the Nanoscience Program and Director of the Center for Materials Science (CMS) at Zewail City, has been awarded the Doctor of Science (D.Sc) degree from Mansoura University. The Doctor of Science (DSC) is an advanced academic degree awarded in recognition of substantial and sustained contributions to scientific knowledge and research. It is often considered a higher doctorate, highly above the level of conventional PhD, and is typically awarded to individuals who have demonstrated a significant international impact on their field.
The research and the overall scientific activity of Professor El-Sherbiny were evaluated by an elite group of British scientists, from prestigious English universities such as University of Cambridge, University of Birmingham, etc, who are experts in nanotechnology and nanopolymer fields, and all of them hold the same esteemed DSc degree:
1- Sir Prof. Mark Welland (FRS, FREng, FIET, FInstP), Head of Nanoscience Centre, University of Cambridge, UK
2- Prof. James Hay (DPhil, DSc, FRSC), University of Birmingham, UK
3- Prof. Andrew Lowe (DPhil, DSc, FRSC), (Currently in a sabbatical at KU, UAE)"

Prof. Ibrahim El-Sherbiny Awarded Doctor of Science Degree (D.Sc.)
June 3, 2024
"Zewail City of Science, Technology and Innovation is pleased to announce that Professor Dr. Ibrahim El-Sherbiny, the Founding Director of the Nanoscience Program and Director of the Center for Materials Science (CMS) at Zewail City, has been awarded the Doctor of Science (D.Sc) degree from Mansoura University. The Doctor of Science (DSC) is an advanced academic degree awarded in recognition of substantial and sustained contributions to scientific knowledge and research. It is often considered a higher doctorate, highly above the level of conventional PhD, and is typically awarded to individuals who have demonstrated a significant international impact on their field.
The research and the overall scientific activity of Professor El-Sherbiny were evaluated by an elite group of British scientists, from prestigious English universities such as University of Cambridge, University of Birmingham, etc, who are experts in nanotechnology and nanopolymer fields, and all of them hold the same esteemed DSc degree:
1- Sir Prof. Mark Welland (FRS, FREng, FIET, FInstP), Head of Nanoscience Centre, University of Cambridge, UK
2- Prof. James Hay (DPhil, DSc, FRSC), University of Birmingham, UK
3- Prof. Andrew Lowe (DPhil, DSc, FRSC), (Currently in a sabbatical at KU, UAE)"

Academics
Discover the Journey with University of Science and Technology
Welcome to the University of Science and Technology at Zewail City,
where excellence meets innovation, as an Egyptian institution with international standards, we pride ourselves on offering world-class education, cutting-edge research, and transformative opportunities. Join us in our mission to push boundaries, shape the future, and make a difference on a global scale.
Welcome to a community of scholars, innovators, and leaders dedicated to opening the potential of science and technology for society.
Welcome to Zewail City.
UST Mission
The mission of the University of Science and Technology (UST) is to serve the people of Egypt through pre-eminence in creating, communicating, and applying knowledge, through science, research, technology, and academic values. UST strives to develop leaders and citizens who challenge the present and enrich the future. UST prepares students to embark on a 21st-century knowledge society guided by our policy: “Admission to the university is only based on merit”. The University is defined by a culture of interdisciplinary teaching and research, coupled with academic rigor. By transcending disciplinary boundaries, we encourage our students, faculty, and staff to tackle complex and vexing challenges facing modern societies at local, national, and global levels. Our programs are responsive to the changing needs of society and relevant to the goals of our students and the needs of the community. Our academic environment is rich in opportunities for independence and collaboration and reflective of the traditions of excellence, innovation, and leadership that the University aims to establish.
This mission is achieved by:
Building a strong foundation in Sciences and Engineering.
Providing the knowledge and skills essential for career and personal success.
Integrating teaching, research, and service in ways that enhance the learning experience.
Supporting a dynamic environment where innovation, openness, and creativity are fostered.
Using advanced technologies to meet the changing educational needs and to establish links with the global community.
Establishing partnerships with business, industry, educational institutions, and government agencies.
UST Philosophy:
The University of Science and Technology is an educational institution that carefully trains selected students in basic state-of-the-art science and engineering and offers them hands-on research opportunities. The total annual number of students at the university does not exceed 5,000. The Supreme Advisory Board must unanimously approve any increases above this limit. The university is equipped with the most advanced tools and laboratories. The campus enjoys self-sufficiency with a real scientific environment to encourage the exchange of ideas and scholarly discourse. Given its independent and nonprofit status, the university policy is guided by merit-based admission, with financial aid provided to students with limited means. The university is intent on preserving our country's national culture and values while cultivating a forward-thinking outlook on the world. The basic academic structure includes both undergraduate and graduate programs with a versatile and multidisciplinary approach.
UST Vision
To be a world class University that plays a pivotal role in addressing strategic challenges on the national and international level. We aim at providing new inventions that will contribute effectively in developing societies and enhancing economies.

School of Engineering
Programs offered by the School of Engineering (ENGR) emphasize hands-on experience, innovation, soft skills, and multidisciplinary education to help graduates cope with the progressively competitive and dynamic professional environment. Program courses have integrated experimental and/or design elements. All ENGR programs culminate in a two-semester senior design project in which students, working in small groups, demonstrate their mastery of the required engineering competencies and the design process. Furthermore, ENGR programs have courses that tackle complex engineering problems which involve wide-ranging and conflicting technical issues, address problems not encompassed by current standards and codes, involve diverse groups of stakeholders, and include many component parts or sub-problems.

Admission & Scholarships
Auditing Courses
Students who audit a course do so for the purposes of self-enrichment and academic exploration.
This is offered for UST students and non-UST students as well.
Auditing a course allows an undergraduate student or an external student to take a class without
the benefit of:
A grade or credit for a course
transcript
Enrolment certificate
Scholarship
Auditing for UST Students
Students are not allowed to audit a course from their academic plan. Courses that are taken to satisfy university requirements, school requirements, and program requirements are not to be audited.
Students are not asked to satisfy the prerequisites or corequisites for audited courses.
Students can only audit theoretical courses, not labs or language classes.
Students are allowed to register for a course as auditing during the drop and add courses week.
Students cannot change from regular enrolment to auditing upon their preferences.
Students may enroll in a maximum of one course per semester.
Auditors are not required to submit any coursework or attend final exams.
Auditors are subject to all conduct, academic integrity, and academic regulations of the university
Auditing for Non-UST Students
Outsiders who are willing to audit UST courses are allowed upon satisfaction of the following conditions:
Fill out an application and submit the most recent education certificate.
Students can only audit theoretical courses, not labs or language classes.
Students are allowed to register for a course as auditing during the drop and add courses week.
Students have to seek the Program Director’s approval to enroll in a class.
Auditors are not entitled to any enrolment certifications, military certifications, or transcripts.
Auditors do not earn academic Credit Hours and receive no final grades.
Auditors are charged for 1 Credit Hour per course plus course fees.
The refund policy applies to auditing students.
Non-degree program
Non-UST Students may apply to register in credit courses through UST Non-degree program for many reasons, inclusive of:
Building professional skills for career advancement.
Test drive” a different field of study
Getting a feel for what UST courses and the UST experience are like
Completing prerequisite courses needed for another program (post graduate studies) in another institution.
The non-degree program is valid for a maximum of 32 Credit Hours/ 2 semesters.
Requirements:
Students must submit the same application credentials as degree-seeking students.
Students should have completed a minimum of one year (32 credits) in another accredited
post-secondary academic institution. The minimum CGPA required to be considered is 3.00.
Students must pass the personal interview.
No scholarship is available for the non-degree students.
Students are to be enrolled in courses based on seat availability.
Non-degree students are entitled for enrolment certificates and transcripts.
Tuition and Fees:
Students pay tuition for credit courses at a per-credit rate announced for the same academic year of their enrolment in the non-degree program.
No scholarships are awarded to non-degree students.

Research Office
Research Office at Zewail City
Our mission in the research office is to create a nurturing research environment where we identify promising opportunities and support pioneering research ideas. The research office is the hub for connecting our researchers to external funding agencies, as well as to ensure implementing the global academic standards and values. We value the highly collaborative and interdisciplinary nature of modern research efforts and therefore aim to provide a highly dynamic environment to support and facilitate the efforts of Zewail City’s researchers.
Research Indicator
Zewail City of Science, Technology and Innovation employs a variety of research indicators to gauge its scholarly impact and scientific productivity in various fields. These indicators encompass quantitative metrics such as publication outputs which are more than 2.6K publications published in high impact factor journals, citation counts (above 47.7 K citations), and H5-index which equals 53 in 2024 based on Scopus database. These indicators provide insights into the volume and influence of the institution’s research output.
Research Policies
Research policies of Zewail City of Science and Technology are guided by our mission to advance scientific understanding in a manner that will benefit Egypt, the region, and humanity. Our research policies serve to promote and facilitate cooperation and maintain the rigorous standards needed to achieve this aim.
Zewail City Ranking
The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a universal call to action by all countries to end poverty, protect the planet, and improve the lives and prospects of everyone, everywhere.
The 17 Goals were adopted by all United Nations Member States in 2015, as part of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.
Funding Opportunity
The research office identifies and disseminates potential funding opportunities, promotes the ethical and responsible conduct of research, ensures compliance with regulatory requirements related to research activities, supports submission of proposals, ensures adherence to governmental and funding agency regulations, follows up on technical and financial reporting of research projects, and promotes the responsible use and sharing of research data. The office also updates and circulates research policies and news, drafts memorandums of understanding, and academic agreements with other institutions to promote interdisciplinary and international collaborations with potential partners.
Research Services and Requests
This section gives an overview of essential services provided by the Research Office to support the research activities at Zewail City such as collecting the recent research progress, research news, reviewing the research proposals submitted by ZC researchers to make sure that they fulfilling all requirements, handling lab notebook requests, and provide a catalog of ZC equipment list to avoid duplication.

Admission & Scholarships
Studying at the University of Science and Technology
Candidates applying to the University of Science and Technology (UST) must read the admission criteria quite well to determine their eligibility to apply UST. This step ensures that the applicant meets the basic requirements set forth by the UST Admission Office to proceed further into the application process. The process requires input from students regarding their type of high school degree, scores in specific subjects, the current overall grade/score, science and math subjects scores, graduation year, school, program, and related information.
Undergraduate Admission Process & Requirements
Candidates applying to the University of Science and Technology (UST) must read the admission criteria quite well to determine their eligibility to apply UST. This step ensures that the applicant meets the basic requirements set forth by the UST Admission Office to proceed further into the application process. The process requires input from students regarding their type of high school degree, scores in specific subjects, the current overall grade/score, science and math subjects scores, graduation year, school, program, and related information
Transfer Students
Undergraduates who are applying for admission to UST and have completed courses at a nationally or internationally accredited college or university other than UST may request an evaluation for the transfer of credit hours. External non-UST courses may be considered for transfer credit if all criteria for assessment are met.
Auditing & Non-degree Programs
Explore our flexible learning opportunities designed especially for you, our auditors and non-degree programs offer you the chance to deepen your knowledge, Join us, and start on a journey of continuous learning and growth.
More Info!
Graduate Admission Process & Requirements
University of Science and Technology at Zewail City (UST) offers graduate programs in Sciences and Engineering disciplines. These graduate programs accept applications from B.Sc. and M.Sc. degree holders.
Applicants with B.Sc. degrees have the opportunity to apply for MEng and M.Sc. graduate programs.
Applicants with M.Sc. degrees have the opportunity to apply for the Ph.D. program. Applicants have to satisfy admission criteria to be accepted in graduate programs at UST.
Tuition & Scholarships
The University of Science and Technology provides a world-class educational environment. Tuition fees are calculated based on the number of registered credit hours in each academic semester. The tuition fees include access to mandatory laboratory supplies for experiments, a library, scientific databases, and career advising services.

Biography
Prof. Tamer Samir Ahmed holds a PhD in Chemical Engineering from North Carolina State University (USA) and earned his BS and MS degrees in Chemical Engineering from Cairo University. He has extensive experience in teaching, research, and professional service, with a strong focus on waste valorization, resource efficiency, process optimization, and reaction engineering. He always believes that sustainable solutions lie at the intersection of science and practicality. Currently, he serves as a Professor in the Chemical Engineering Department at Cairo University and in the Environmental Engineering Program at Zewail City. He has taught a wide range of courses, including Mass Transfer Operations, Reaction Engineering, Material and Energy Balances, Safety and Risk Analysis, Polymerization Reaction Engineering, Polymer Science and Engineering, Cryogenic Processes, Natural Gas Processing, and Chemical Engineering Laboratory. In addition to his teaching responsibilities, he has supervised over 17 senior graduation projects and guided more than 25 MS and PhD students in their research endeavors so far. He has also served as a reviewer for various scientific journals and entities, solidifying his position as an active contributor to the scientific community. His professional experience spans across various industries, from micro- to large-sized enterprises in diverse industrial sectors. He has worked as the Chief Technical Advisor on projects with esteemed entities such as the Egypt National Cleaner Production Center, the UNIDO, and the AfDB. He has contributed to over 60 cleaner production, resource efficiency, chemicals and chemical waste management, waste valorization, and environmental assessment studies, which were part of 16 international projects funded by major donors like UNIDO, UNEP, AfDB, GiZ, EU, and others. Furthermore, he has served as a trainer in numerous technical capacity-building activities with UNIDO, UNEP, and the EU. He joined Zewail City in September 2017 and has been the Program Director of the Environmental Engineering Program since July 2021. As the Accreditation Champion then as the Program Director, the program achieved ABET Accreditation, highlighting its commitment to excellence.

Biography
Dr. Ahmed Eltaweel joined Zewail City of Science and Technology in 2017. Dr. Eltaweel has more than twenty years of academic research experience in the thermo-fluidic field, involving basic research and applied engineering-funded projects in Fluids/Aerodynamics/Propulsion and Gas Cycles applications’ modeling, design, and development including five years of industrial and experimental expertise in thermal management and outdoor cooling applications. Dr. Eltaweel received his Ph.D. from the Aerospace and Mechanical Engineering Department, University of Notre Dame, USA. He received his Bachelor's and Master's in propulsion from the Aerospace and Aeronautical Engineering Department at Cairo University, Egypt. Biography
Amgad El-Deib’s area of expertise lie in modeling and control of renewable energy and energy storage in power systems. Dr. El-Deib received his BSc and MSc from Cairo University, Egypt in 2002 and 2005 respectively and his Ph.D. from the University of Toronto, Canada in 2010 all in electrical engineering with specialization in power systems and power electronics. From 2011 until 2013, he worked as a power system engineer with the Renewable Power Group at the consulting firm Hatch Limited in Toronto. Afterwards Dr. El-Deib joined the wind turbine manufacturer ENERCON in the Research and Development department. He got involved with different R&D projects focusing on power system integration of ENERCON’s wind power plants. In January 2015, Dr. El-Deib returned to Cairo University in Egypt as an Assistant Professor where he would go on to advise New and Renewable Energy Authority (NREA) to develop core procedures for grid integration of renewable power plants to the national grid of Egypt. Later he consulted for Orascom Construction in the engineering of the 250 MW BOO wind power plant in the Gulf of Suez. Since September 2017, Dr. El-Deib has been with the Renewable Energy Engineering Program at the University of Science and Technology at Zewail City as well as leading the development of the Smart Grid and energy storage labs

Biography
Dr. Doaa Shawky serves as a professor within the School of Computational Sciences and Artificial Intelligence. She embarked on her academic journey at Cairo University, obtaining a Bachelor of Science in electronics and electrical communications engineering with honors in 1996 from the Faculty of Engineering. Her pursuit of knowledge led her to acquire a Master of Science in database analysis and design in 2000 and a Doctor of Philosophy in software engineering in 2005. Professionally, Shawky commenced her career immediately after her undergraduate studies in 1996, joining the Electronic Research Institute as a research assistant. She then expanded her skillset, taking on roles as a system analyst and software developer. Between 1998 and 2005, she joined Cairo University as an instructor and teaching assistant, progressing to the roles of assistant and associate professor, and eventually attaining her current position as a professor at the same institution. In 2013, Shawky began her affiliation with ZC, contributing as an instructor and researcher at the Center of Learning Technologies. After departing in 2018, she returned to ZC in 2022, taking on the role of director for the Software Development Program within the School of Computational Sciences and Artificial Intelligence. Dr. Shawky's research interests are broad, encompassing software engineering, cloud computing, computational intelligence, machine learning, and adaptive e-learning. She is an active participant in the international conference circuit, often featured as an invited speaker and coach. Her professional memberships reflect her commitment to her field, including affiliations with the IEEE Computer Society, the IEEE Cloud Computing Community, the IEEE Software Technical Council, the International Association of Computer Science and Information Technology, and the International Association of Engineers

Biography
Dr. Hatem Fayed is currently an associate professor and serves as the director of the applied mathematics program at the University of Science and Technology at Zewail City. Fayed’s academic background started in 1993 when he received his Bachelor of Science degree in electronics and electrical engineering from the Faculty of Engineering at Cairo University. He received his Master of Science and Doctor of Philosophy degrees in engineering mathematics from the same university. Prior to joining Zewail City, Fayed worked as a freelance software engineer for the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO). In 1993 he was appointed at the Faculty of Engineering at Cairo University as a teaching assistant and then as an assistant professor in 2005. In 2013, he was appointed as an associate professor at the Faculty of Engineering at Cairo University. Fayed joined Zewail City as a full-time faculty at the applied mathematics program at Zewail City in 2014. In recognition of his teaching efforts, Fayed received several awards such as: the distinguished teacher award by IEEE – Student Branch at Zewail City in 2015, the most distinguished professor award from Zewail City in 2016, the excellence in teaching award from Zewail City in 2018. Fayed’s research efforts are related to: machine learning, time series forecasting, deep learning, optimization techniques and computer vision. In this context, he participated in two research projects funded by NTRA and ITAC and published 22 research articles that have received more than 400 citations.

Biography
Dr Mohamed Farhat is a full professor, Director of Center for Nanotechnology, and Director of Nanotechnology and Nanoelectronics Engineering Program at Zewail City of Science and Technology. He is also a founding member of Center for Photonics and Smart Materials (CPSM) at ZC. Dr Farhat is working in the area of computational modeling and optimization of photonic devices including optical sensors, plasmonics, metamaterial, photonic crystal fibers, polarization handling devices, SOI, and solar cells. Based on his research work, 200 journal papers, mostly in IEEE and IEE journals, have been published, and 150 conference papers have been presented in the best national and international meetings.
Additionally, two books have been published by the world-leading scientific publishers; Wiley (Computational Liquid Crystal Photonics: Fundamentals, Modelling and Applications, 2016) and Springer (Computational Photonic Sensors, 2018). He managed to secure funds in the region of 45 MEGP from STDF and ASRT as a PI or CO-PI. Dr Farhat is member of the Specialized Scientific Council of the Fundamental Science Research at Academy of Scientific Research and Technology (ASRT) (2018-2021, 2021-2024), member of the National Committee of Physics at ASRT (2018-2021, vice president 2022-2025) and member of the National Committee for Education, Science and Culture at ASRT (2022- 2025). He got many national/international awards including best PhD thesis and Mansoura Incentive Awards from Mansoura University for the years 2011/2012, and 2014, respectively. In addition, he was awarded the Incentive State Award for Engineering Sciences from the Egyptian Government along with the African Union- The World Academy of Sciences (AU-TWAS) Young Scientists National Award in Basic Science, Technology and Innovation, for the year 2014/2015. Further, he received the Shrouk Academy award for scientific innovation in electronic engineering and applications (2015/2016). Additionally, he got the Medal of Excellence from the Egyptian President (August 2017) and Venice Kamel Award for scientific creativity for young researchers in the field of technological innovations (2016/2017) and Amin Lotfy prize for physical science (2017/2018). Dr Farhat has also been awarded Shoman prize for computational physics (2021), State Prize for Excellence in Engineering Sciences from the ASRT, Khalifa Award for Distinguished Arab professors (2022) and Zewail City Awards of Excellence in Scientific Research (July 2023) and Excellence in Teaching (July 2024).

Biography
Dr. Nagwa El-Badri is the Founding Chair of the Biomedical Sciences Program, and the Director of the Center of Excellence for Stem Cells and Regenerative Medicine at Zewail City of Science and Technology. Dr. Nagwa received her medical degree with honors from Cairo University College of Medicine and her Ph.D. degree with distinction from the College of Medicine, University of South Florida (USF), USA. Her postdoctoral training as a Fellow of the American Heart Association was in the field of stem cell biology. She was awarded an Initial Investigator ship Award from the American Heart Association, and received research support from the Science and Technology Development Fund, the Academy of Scientific Research and Technology, the American Heart Association, the National Institutes of Health, and several other foundations. She contributed more than 100 publications and books in the field of stem cell biology and regenerative medicine. Prior to joining Zewail City, Dr. Nagwa El-Badri was the founder and Director of the Undergraduate and Graduate Program of Women’s Health at USF. She also established the graduate program in Aging and Neuroscience, and the Medicine and Gender Scholarly Concentration at the College of Medicine, USF. At Zewail City, she was awarded a prestigious grant from the Science and Technology Development Fund to establish the Center of Excellence for Stem Cells and Regenerative Medicine (CESC)at Zewail City. The CESC was recently selected to receive a second large prestigious award for successful STDF-funded centers of excellence. She developed several undergraduate and graduate programs both at Zewail City and USF. Her graduate students at both institutes received many national and international awards, and several publications were awarded first place and gold medals in national and international conferences. She received numerous awards for her efforts in research and in promoting women in science. She is recipient of the Women Appreciation Award from the Egyptian Academy of Scientific Research and Technology for 2020. She was selected for AAMC Mid-Career Women Faculty Professional Development, USA, and was awarded Exemplary Leader in Management Position for Global Women in Stem, Meera Kaul Foundation. She is an elected Fellow of the American College of Nutrition, and served on several national and international committees including the Arab League Education and Cultural Organization Research Committee, the Higher Education Reform Experts Committee, and the Council for Education and Scientific Research Policies at the Academy of Scientific Research, and the National Council for Women

Biography
Samy S. Soliman received his B.Sc. and M.Sc. (with honor) degrees from the Electronics and Electrical Communications Engineering Department, Faculty of Engineering, Cairo University, Egypt, in 2007 and 2009, respectively. He received his Ph.D. degree from the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department, University of Alberta in 2014. He was awarded the Cairo University prize of Engineer Nabil El-Gebaly and the Cairo University prize of Engineer Reda, both in 2008.
Dr. Soliman started as an Assistant Lecturer at the Electronics and Electrical Communications Department, Cairo University, as well as a Research and Teaching Assistant at the Electronics Engineering Department, American University in Cairo (AUC). He joined the AITF/iCORE Wireless Communications Laboratory (iWCL) in 2009 as a Research Assistant and worked as a Post-Secondary Teaching Assistant at the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department, University of Alberta, Canada. He held a Post-Doctoral Research Fellowship at the University of British Columbia, Canada. Dr. Soliman is currently an Associate Professor at the Communications and Information Engineering Program, Zewail City of Science and Technology, Egypt.
Dr. Soliman has held the position of director of information technology at Zewail City and served as the deputy coordinator of the professional master’s program in telecommunications at Cairo University. His expertise lies in information and communication technology, with a focus on telecommunication networks. He has authored approximately 40 publications, supervised more than 40 postgraduate students and actively participated in research projects related to wireless communication systems and ICT industry transformation.
Dr. Soliman's research interests include 5G/6G Communication Systems, Wireless Sensor Networks, Wireless Cooperative Networks, Multiple Input Multiple Output (MIMO) Systems, and Ultra-wide Bandwidth Wireless Systems.

Biography
Professor Tarek Ibrahim is a professor of physics at the University of Science and Technology. He is also the director of the physics of universe PU program. He became the Dean of Academic Affairs at ZC since 2017.
Ibrahim started his academic life in 1985 when he received his Bachelor of Science degree, excellent with honor, from the faculty of Science, Alexandria University.
In 1991 he earned his first Master of Science degree in solid state physics from the same school.
Professor Ibrahim joined Northeastern University NEU, Boston, MA, USA in 1992 where he obtained his second Master degree in Physics in 1995, and then he received his Doctor of Philosophy degree PhD in High Energy Physics HEP from the same school.
In 2011, he received his Doctor of Science degree DSc from Alexandria University upon the recommendation of three Fellows of Royal Society FRS of London, UK. Ibrahim began his career as a teaching assistant at Alexandria University in 1985, then an assistant lecturer in 1991. In 1998, he held the position of assistant professor and the position of associate professor in 2003. He was appointed as a full professor in 2009 in the same physics department of the Faculty of Science, Alexandria University.
Professor Ibrahim joined Northeastern University NEU as a visiting scholar in the years 1999 and 2000. He visited NEU as an assistant professor from 2002 to 2004. He then moved to Beirut as a visiting associate professor at The American University of Beirut AUB for one year, 2005-5006. From 2012-2014 he held the position of physics department chairman at Beirut Arab University BAU.
He joined the American University of Cairo AUC in 2011 and Arab Academy for Science and Technology many years as a visiting assistant, associate and full professor.
Education
B.Sc. (Physics) Excellent with honor (1985), Alexandria University, Alexandria, Egypt.
Master of Science (Physics) (1991), Alexandria University, Alexandria, Egypt.
MS. (Physics) (1995), Northeastern University, Boston, MA, USA.
PhD (Physics) (1998), Northeastern University, Boston, MA, USA.
Research Focus Area
The principal area of Prof. Ibrahim research is the phenomenology of N = 1 supersymmetric theories. Within this supersymmetric framework several topics have been explored. These include explicit CP and T violations arising as a consequence of large phases from the complex couplings in the soft supersymmetry breaking sector of supergravity. Implications of these for a wide range of SUSY phenomenology were investigated. These include the electric dipole moments of the electron, the muon, tauneutrino, top quark and the neutron, direct detection and density of dark matter, anomalous magnetic moment of muon, proton lifetime, Higgs masses and mixings, decay of B0 mesons, squark decays, neutral and charged Higgs couplings and decays and radiative corrections of third generation fermions masses. The vector like generation models as extensions of MSSM with their effects on the electric and chromo electric dipole moments of the top quark, neutron, the tau lepton and the neutrinos are discussed. Their effects on the taudecay into muon and photon, the muon decay into an electron and a photon and the magnetic dipole moments of leptons and neutrinos are considered.
Awards
Professor Tarek Ibrahim has received several awards. He obtained Mahmoud Elsherbiny Award in Physics in 1986. He was awarded the outstanding professor award presented by Engineering Department at Northeastern University for the year 2005. He was awarded the State of Egypt Prize in Physical Sciences from the Academy of Scientific Research and Technology for the year 2001. He was awarded the Alexandria University Encouraging Prize in Physics for the year 2001. He was awarded the Shoman Prize in Physics, an Arab world level prize, received from Jordan for the year 2000. He was awarded the Lawrence Award presented by Physics Department at Northeastern University for excellence in teaching for the years 1997 and 1998. He was awarded the prize for the most cited paper in Alexandria University according to Scopuscitation database in 2010. Finally he was awarded the most cited researchers’ award and the publication award presented by Misr Elkheir Foundation for the year 2011.

Dr. Tamer Ashour Ali is an accomplished researcher and academic leader with extensive experience in industry and academia. He earned his Ph.D. in Electrical and Computer Engineering from Rice University in 2010 and has held various leadership roles in both fields.
Currently, Dr. Ali serves as the Acting Dean of Strategic Enrollment Management and Professor in the Communications and Information Engineering Program. In his role as Acting Dean, he oversees institutional strategies for student recruitment, retention, and academic program development, leveraging data-driven decision-making, digital transformation, and AI-driven analytics to enhance educational outcomes and operational efficiency.
Dr. Ali leads the university's continuous improvement processes and short-to-long term action plans, utilizing technology-based tools to drive efficiency. He coordinates the Learning Outcome assessment processes across all programs, leads the efforts for seeking and sustaining international accreditation, coordinate the work with the Learning Technologies Services Office, and oversees the Teaching Effectiveness Office, which develops the skills of the teaching faculty and assistants.
Dr. Ali's research focuses on electromagnetic sensing, nanophotonics, Frequency Selective Surfaces, computational methods, AIoT applications, and software development. His work has been widely recognized, and he has extensive experience managing technical teams and developing advanced sensor systems, particularly in Wireless Sensor Networks, Embedded Systems, and the Internet of Things.
